metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1471
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 7: Netrin-1 regulates excitatory synapse number and size. WT cortical incubated with netrin-1 for 24 h show increased Vglut1 and PSD95 positive synapse number and size while incubation with netrin-function blocking (nfb) antibody decreases synapse number and size.
Fig 8: mEPSC frequency and amplitude are increase upon netrin-1 incubation, while decreased upon nfb application. Netrin -/- neurons show decreased mEPSC frequency and amplitude. DCC -/- (netrin-1 receptor) neurones also show decreased mESC frequency but no effect on mEPSC amplitude.

24/10/2017 Pim
- Addition of netrin-1-coated microspheres results in local enrichment of pre-
and postsynaptic proteins at contact sites (Fig.5/6). Experimental description: Both male and female rats and mice were used at the ages specified in the text. All wild-type and transgenic mice were a CD1 genetic background. All rat studies used the Sprague Dawley strain. Ntn1-/ and Dcc-/ mice were obtained from Marc Tessier-Lavigne (Rockefeller University, NY) and Robert Weinberg (Harvard University, MA), respectively.

24/10/2017 Pim
- Species of recombinant netrin-1 traced back to chicken via PMID:8062384. Literal: "An E10 chick brain cDNA library and an E25 chick spinal cord library were screened with probes designed from these fragments as well as from cDNAs isolated as the screen proceeded. "
- I approved of the mouse species annotation based on the data on the Netrin-KO animals in Fig.8.
